summary_one_line: Input must carry a file-format magic that fails the ELF/MachO/WASM probes yet matches a later (PE) probe, reaching the 1556 true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose leading magic bytes pass all three earlier format probes as non-matches (not ELF, not MachO, not WebAssembly's `\0asm`) so object_file is still null at line 1556, while being close enough to a recognizable format (PE/COFF) that TryOpenPEFile is exercised. Concretely: bytes at offsets 0x00-0x03 NOT equal to the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d, instead drifting toward other format magics (e.g. 4c 46 / 7f 45 4c 4c 46 / 01 01 ...).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-WASM leading bytes (seed 3 starts 4c 46 'LF', seed 4 starts 7f 45 4c 4c 46, seed 1 starts 01 01) and the per-role overlay marks lines 1557-1558 (TryOpenPEFile) as [W] winner-only and shows the blocker at L1556 taking T=60 F=0 for the winner, i.e. the winner repeatedly reaches and takes the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser-blocking seed begins 00 61 73 6d (the WebAssembly '\0asm' magic), so object_file becomes non-null at line 1552 (TryOpenWebAssemblyFile) and line 1556's true branch is never taken (winner W L1556 = T=60 F=0 vs loser L L1556 = T=0 F=120). The byte-diff confirms losers are pinned to a-s-m at offsets 0x01-0x03.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP feedback: the integer/string magic-byte comparisons inside each TryOpen*File probe emit a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket, so inputs that get progressively closer to (or away from) a given format magic are preserved as new corpus. This gradient lets value_profile steer leading bytes away from the WASM-magic basin that traps naive and toward a downstream-format magic, exposing the 1556 true branch. naive has only edge-hit feedback and cannot sense partial magic-byte proximity, so it stays stuck on whatever magic its corpus already matched.

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair at this branch is value_profile beating naive with the value_profile technique delta.
The blocked (winning) side is the true branch of the line-1556 null check, which leads to TryOpenPEFile.
Lines 1557-1558 (TryOpenPEFile) are exercised only by the winner.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side every time (T=60 F=0) while the loser never does (T=0 F=120).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1556  T=60 F=0  T=0 F=120  if (!object_file.get()) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: All loser-blocking seeds begin with the WebAssembly magic 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm'), satisfying the earlier WASM probe before line 1556.
Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-WASM leading bytes (e.g. an ELF-like 7f 45 4c 4c 46 prefix).
The byte diff shows losers pinned to the s/m of '\0asm' at offsets 0x02-0x03 while winners diverge.
value_profile's distinguishing feedback is the CMP_MAP distance bucket that rewards inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching, which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage dash-row check does not apply. The value_profile MECHANISM CONTEXT explicitly states 'No I2SRandReplace' and that observed mutation_op are 'havoc/token names only', so an I2S substitution attribution would be unsupported; the divergence is attributed to value_profile's CMP_MAP distance feedback per the per-role coverage and byte-diff evidence above.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same chained magic-byte probes where value_profile resolves the post-WASM branch no faster than naive (no separation in time-to-resolve), indicating CMP_MAP proximity feedback gives no advantage over plain edge coverage for steering leading magic bytes.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the winner is steered specifically toward the PE magic is indirect: the [W] tag on TryOpenPEFile shows it is reached, but the prompt does not show a successful PE parse, so the winner may instead be reaching 1556-true via inputs that simply fail all probes (and hit THROWF at 1561, also [W]).
